subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI) + Korean official (Statistics Korea, Bank of Korea) + independent press.
NEGATIVE-SKEW case: the world's lowest fertility (~0.75, a locked-in population decline) plus a demonstrated institutional shock (the Dec-2024 martial-law crisis) dominate the tail.
Source-lean: moderate-to-high Anglophone on the political crisis and North Korea; economic/demographic numbers from Korean/multilateral primaries.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May — the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A credible central bank and an export-tech powerhouse, against ageing-driven fiscal pressure and very high household debt.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~48% GDP, crossing 50% in the 2026 budget; the IMF projects ~59% by 2030 on ageing/pension pressure. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: An independent, credible Bank of Korea (2% inflation target). (Bank of Korea)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The won is freely traded but not a major reserve currency; no exorbitant privilege. (BoK)

inflation.notes: Heavy crude/LNG import dependence via Hormuz drives a near-term energy-price/inflation shock. (Reuters)

banking_stability.notes: The BoK repeatedly flags very high household debt and housing imbalances as the key stability risk; macroprudential tightening underway. (Bank of Korea FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A semiconductor/auto/shipbuilding export power, no resource curse; a 2025 US deal caps tariffs at 15% but heavy China and US exposure remains. (CRS)

institutional.notes: Strong courts and high state capacity that functioned through the crisis; press freedom slipped sharply.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong courts (which upheld the impeachment 8-0), though chaebol entanglement and politicized-prosecution dynamics persist. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible Statistics Korea / Bank of Korea data. (Statistics Korea)

civil_service_capacity.notes: High competence; institutions functioned through the crisis. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: RSF rank fell sharply to ~61st (2025) on ownership/defamation pressure, losing regional-leader status. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Institutions self-corrected after a martial-law shock, against intense polarization and a locked-in fertility collapse.

political_stability.notes: The Dec-2024 martial-law shock, Yoon's impeachment/removal, and a June-2025 snap election; institutions self-corrected, but the precedent and polarization are now demonstrated risks. (NPR)

civil_liberties.notes: A strong baseline, with some narrowing of expression noted. (WJP 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Work/E-visas and a points-based skilled track make entry feasible, but language scoring, capped quotas, and a thin path to belonging/citizenship are real ceilings. (Korea Immigration Service)

social_cohesion.notes: Intense gender/generational polarization (a stark young-men-vs-women split) offset by high social order. (Carnegie)

demographics.notes: The world's lowest fertility (~0.75); population declining yearly from 2025, projected ~-30% by 2072. A locked-in negative skew. (Statistics Korea)

geopolitical.notes: A core US treaty ally facing a persistent North Korea standoff.

alliance_reliability.notes: A core US treaty ally (defense spend +7.5% FY2026), with some tariff/cost-sharing friction. (CRS)

conflict_involvement.notes: A North Korea nuclear/artillery standoff and 'hostile two states' doctrine; a persistent live tail risk. (CRS; UN)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account, G7-aligned, no domestic controls. (observed)

physical_practical.notes: World-class infrastructure, healthcare, and very low crime, against ageing-driven cost strain.

climate.notes: High-capacity and resilient - intensifying heat, typhoons, and coastal flooding keep it modestly negative. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Excellent universal NHI with strong outcomes; ageing-driven cost strain (reserves projected to deplete ~2030). (OECD)

disaster_insurance.notes: Moderate seismic, typhoon/flood exposure with strong state response capacity. (observed)

summary: An export-tech powerhouse with strong courts and state capacity, world-class infrastructure and healthcare, very low crime, and a credible central bank. But the outcome distribution skews negative: the world's lowest fertility (a locked-in population decline), a demonstrated institutional shock (the 2024 martial-law crisis), very high household debt, a persistent North Korea standoff, and a thin path to belonging for foreigners.
